data.title: Ukrainian Deputy Mayor's Son Murdered in Vienna Over Cryptocurrency
data.description: According to Odaily, Vienna police have confirmed the violent murder of 21-year-old Danylo K., the son of Kharkiv's Deputy Mayor Sergey K. The motive is suspected to be related to his cryptocurrency assets. Two Ukrainian suspects, aged 19 and 45, allegedly forced the victim to surrender his crypto wallet password before beating him to death and burning his body. Following the crime, the suspects fled to Ukraine. The case has been transferred to Ukrainian judicial authorities at Austria's request.

data.title: Ethereum Spot ETFs in the U.S. See Significant Inflows
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Farside Investors reported a net inflow of $140 million into U.S. Ethereum spot ETFs. The breakdown of the inflows includes $53 million into BlackRock's ETHA, $34.4 million into Fidelity's FETH, $4.5 million into Bitwise's ETHW, $27.6 million into Grayscale's ETHE, and $20.7 million into Grayscale's Mini ETH.

data.title: Russia's Bitcoin Mining Gains Attention as an Export Asset
data.description:
According to Odaily, Russian President Vladimir Putin's economic advisor, Maxim Oreshkin, has suggested that crypto assets should be included in Russia's balance of payments statistics. He highlighted that Bitcoin mining is becoming an undervalued export project for the country. Oreshkin noted that some Russian companies are using crypto assets for cross-border payments and selling mined Bitcoin for profit, which has a tangible impact on the foreign exchange market.

Data from Luxor Technology indicates that Russia currently accounts for nearly 16% of the global hash rate, ranking second only to the United States. Sergey Bezdelov, Chairman of the Russian Industrial Mining Association, stated that Russian miners produced approximately 55,000 BTC in 2023. However, due to the upcoming halving, production is expected to decrease to around 35,000 BTC in 2024.

data.title: Kalshi, Robinhood, Crypto.com Hit With Connecticut Stop Order for Gambling Violations
data.description:

source: https://cryptonews.com/news/kalshi-robinhood-cryptocom-connecticut-stop-order-gambling-violations/

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints $500 Million on Solana Blockchain
data.description: According to PANews, on-chain data reveals that at 12:06 and 12:07 (UTC+8), the USDC Treasury minted 250 million USDC on the Solana blockchain in two separate transactions, totaling $500 million.
